evidence.decolonization: Avoids unchanged-pagan-code and single-canonical-style claims, distinguishes archaeological ceramic eggs from preserved bird-egg and modern practice evidence, and frames the 2024 UNESCO Ukraine-Estonia inscription without dissolving Ukrainian authorship.
evidence.pedagogy: C1+ seminar prose separates wax-resist technique, egg-name distinctions, motif systems, regional schools, Easter gift exchange, museum and diaspora layers, with inline and workbook tasks grounded in dossier evidence.
evidence.review: Claude Opus 4.8 independent review found one blocker, the unattested vocabulary item pysancharka. The item and prose occurrence were removed/replaced with attested pysankarka, MDX was regenerated, and follow-up local validators and scans confirmed no matches remain.
evidence.source_policy: Dossier-supported claims only; no invented museum objects, images, artifact IDs, workshop sequences, or popular sayings. Two early-modern Easter fragments were verified under Antolohiia chunk 21c791c4_c0098 before embedding and marked as corpus-checked.
